title: Why Posting Daily Won't Grow Your Channel

transcript.text: I'm going to expose the [music] worst tips that send YouTubers down the wrong path. For example, one common tip is post [music] every single day to grow your channel. But is that really the best way to go? A lot of new YouTubers feel like they [music] need to pump out content on a daily basis. But when you want to make videos that are better for your audience, quality content, then [music] posting as frequently as humanly possible doesn't make as much sense. Let's look at it this way. Sure, daily posting can build momentum when you're making [music] quick, short content. But the game does change when it comes to longer, more thoughtful and considered [music] video. Making high-quality content every single day is tough. Making a video every single day just for the [music] sake of it without even thinking about whether it's good or not for your viewers. For just a moment, let's stop [music] making videos and think about how we make those videos.
